2026-04-23T03:57:56.2185564Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2186142Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2186580Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2186959Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2187288Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2187534Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2187701Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2187892Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2188070Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2188311Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2188525Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2188832Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:57:56.2189119Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. section** — The README has the section heading but no content; provisioning libraries don\\u0026#39;t require an `AzureCredential` in the same way but should note the `azd` / `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` usage pattern\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — No links to related samples, Azure documentation for Application Gateway for Containers, or the `Azure.Provisioning` README\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource but the README Examples section has no example or mention of it; a snippet showing how to create and attach a security policy to a traffic controller is missing\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` (edit)\\n\\n1. **Fill in \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty body under `### Authenticate the Client` with content similar to the pattern used in other provisioning packages (e.g., `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\nThis library provisions Azure infrastructure using [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication is handled automatically by `azd` during deployment. For local development or CI/CD, you can use `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` or any other credential from the [`Azure.Identity`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Identity) package.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Fill in \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — Replace the empty body under `## Next steps` with useful links:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- [Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n- [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n- [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n3. **Add an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` example** — After the existing `### Create a Traffic Controller` example, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Create a Security Policy\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and associate it with a Traffic Controller.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n**File to create:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (edit — add a new test method)\\n\\nAdd the following method to the existing `BasicServiceNetworkingTests` class, below `CreateTrafficController()`:\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest()\\n{\\n    return new Trycep().Define(\\n        ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n            TrafficController controller =\\n                new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                {\\n                    Tags = { [\\u0026#34;environment\\u0026#34;] = \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34; },\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(controller);\\n\\n            ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy =\\n                new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                {\\n                    Parent = controller,\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(policy);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            return infra;\\n        });\\n}\\n\\n[Test]\\npublic async Task CreateSecurityPolicy()\\n{\\n    await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest();\\n    // Expected Bicep output verified by test runner\\n    test.Compare(\\n        \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n        `@description`(\\u0026#39;The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.\\u0026#39;)\\n        param location string = resourceGroup().location\\n\\n        resource controller \\u0026#39;Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01\\u0026#39; = {\\n          name: take(\\u0026#39;controller-\\\\$\\\\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}\\u0026#39;, 24)\\n          location: location\\n        }\\n\\n        resource policy \\u0026#39;Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/securityPolicies@2025-01-01\\u0026#39; = {\\n          name: take(\\u0026#39;policy-\\\\$\\\\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}\\u0026#39;, 24)\\n          location: location\\n          parent: controller\\n        }\\n        \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the exact Bicep resource type path for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` by inspecting `src/Generated/ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.cs` and its `ResourceVersions` inner class, then adjust the `test.Compare(...)` expected string accordingly.\\n\\n**Snippet name:** `ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy`\\n**README location:** After the closing ` ``` ` of the `ServiceNetworkingBasic` snippet block, under `## Examples`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` currently has these sections:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — needs content |\\n| Key concepts | ✅ Present |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Partial — missing security policy example |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ❌ Empty — needs links |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. check `git diff` afterwards to confirm README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-23T04:01:16.2037619Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T03:59:07.86876993Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":3292095671,\"number\":51705,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable\\u003cCosmosDBAccountCapability\\u003e\",\"body\":\"## Background\\nThis is a \\\"sub-issue\\\" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).\\n\\n## Request\\nIt would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable\\u003cCosmosDBAccountCapability\\u003e`.
